item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, this is an SCP that was made by the foundation? Why would they make something they would need to then contain? It just makes no sense to me. Also, everything about this is cliched and the article doesn't do much to make it interesting aside from a joke addendum that's just yet another cliche.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral vote from me. The description hasn't aged all that well with the backstory being frontloaded like that.

But damn if I didn't laugh at the imagery of those tests.
I like this a lot as a twist on a classic trope. It's mind control that works exactly as intended, yet isn't really useful, for a reason that feels like in hindsight like it should be obvious.

item.title: Re: 2 problems
item.content:encoded:
You never explained how the program cancels conscience thoughts. Your brain uses electrical signals to give out commands, remember? That means that you would somehow have to use the auditory section of the brain to emit a signal that somehow intercedes the other electrical signal and turns it into a nothing, like dark matter interacting with normal matter, or a value of -3 being added to a value of 3. It makes no sense.

… and that's what makes it anomalous. It shouldn't be able to cancel neural activity through sound alone, yet it does.

item.title: 2 problems
item.content:encoded:
1: This isn't an scp, this is a science experiment, nothing anomalous about it.
2: You never explained how the program cancels conscience thoughts. Your brain uses electrical signals to give out commands, remember? That means that you would somehow have to use the auditory section of the brain to emit a signal that somehow intercedes the other electrical signal and turns it into a nothing, like dark matter interacting with normal matter, or a value of -3 being added to a value of 3. It makes no sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only person that noticed that 4402F never woke up? The tests are all done on one person after that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I greatly enjoy the test log. Reminds me of making lab procedures for making a pbj in middle school. That said, the description's tone starts out almost like a tale, and probably should be worked on.

I disagree that it should be EXed, as I assume that despite its effect being scientifically explained, it was never stated that it was made by normal means. Might be one of Dr. Crow's cross-testing experiments of old.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted. What it is isn't even stated until the third line of the second paragraph in the description. It also doesn't feel like its really all that anomalous.

I like the tone of the addenda, but it doesn't save it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted because of addendum, hahaha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neutral Vote. It's a decent concept, and needed for OLYMPIA if that's still around, but there's one bit in the containment procedure that bugs the shit out of me:

Audio-output peripherals for this LAN are to be contained within an observation chamber surrounded with noise-canceling vacuum insulation.

Sound waves (or any other kind of pressure waves) don't travel through a vacuum. Noise cancelling is typically a function of delivering inverse-amplitude sound waves to produce destructive interference. "Noise-cancelling vacuum" is a product of the Department of Redundancy Department.

item.title: Re: Eh
item.content:encoded: Thats a good point. It even explains the science in the note. There is nothing anomalous about this at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted. It is -EX, and that assuming it was SCP ever to begin with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, while the addendum was funny, I don't feel this deserves to be a full-fledged SCP to begin with, considering that not only did we make it, but we more or less understand how it works. Sure, it's dangerous, but unlike other potentially-weaponizable SCPs like 044, this isn't anomalous in any way. We were just lucky in making a sound that hypnotizes any listeners.

item.title: Re: Another point
item.content:encoded: The author's not around, so if you want, you have my permission to make those changes.

item.title: Yeah, the current stuff is pretty goddamn stupid.
Another point
item.content:encoded:
The containment on this is grotesquely overblown.

It's a computer program. A computer program that can only have its anomalous effects when it is run. Why do we need to keep it on a computer at all, much less a LAN? I mean, if you have to keep it on a computer, then obviously you don't want it on the Internet so you'd keep it on a LAN, but holy shit explosives that go off if you mistype your password three times and deaf guards ?!

Shit. Look, just keep the program on a CD-ROM, and keep the CD-ROM in a locked box. Make a few copies of the disk, keep them separately. Only run it if you have written permission from site command for approved experimentation. Don't connect the computer to any network at all. Use TEMPEST shielding to prevent Van Eck phreaking. Only test it in a soundproofed room, with the experimenter on the outside and the D-class on the inside.

Format the hard drive at the end of every testing session, replace the CD-ROM in its locked box.

Simple, yes?

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Done.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Safe!

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Sure why not.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Safe sounds good to me.

item.title: Re: Eh
item.content:encoded:
hm

Good question…

I think I was trying to get at the fact that the Foundation understands this SCP perfectly, since we made it, and therefore it's Explained.

And for the record, this is Safe.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: This is Safe. I would support adjusting the classification as needed.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Voct isn't the only one to question this object class.

Quik proposed changing this to Euclid or Safe a long time ago, but we didn't really have a mechanism for that at the time. Now we do.

Fellow staff/mods/admins: Thoughts on changing this classification?

item.title: object class
item.content:encoded: Why is this Keter? It seems perfectly Safe — just don't run the program and don't let anyone else have a copy.

item.title: Re: Eh
item.content:encoded:
Maybe this ought to be moved to -EX now that -EX is a thing? It fits the criteria perfectly.

I know this is a delayed reply, but…

…what?

item.title: Eh
item.content:encoded: Maybe this ought to be moved to -EX now that -EX is a thing? It fits the criteria perfectly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Creating SCPs is one thing… Creating KETER SCPs is another thing entirely…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this SCP overall, and I am used to Foundation researchers creating SCPs (Cassy, for instance), but I don't think a military weapons development program is appropriate. The Addendum is funny, but I feel the implied sadism works against the well-written article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Revealed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i doubt this was intentional. i suppose they predicted a solid block of copper. when observed with a microscope, reviled trillions of clockwork connections, or some shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like the idea of the Foundation intentionally creating new SCP objects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Changed from Keter to Euclid. I considered making it Safe, but the program's not yet a finished product.

[Edit:] Reverted to Keter before the Mass Edit. Suggest change to Euclid when the time comes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, wow. That's hillarious. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Addendum-01 made me giggle like a small child. The rest is great, and sheds a little light on how SCP may operate. Still, I liked the humor delivered absolutely deadpan in the midst of a very serious entry. Well done.
